content.problemStatement:
For an array BBB of MMM **distinct** positive integers, we define a function F(B)F(B)F(B) as follows:


Initialise an empty array CCC and mark all indices of BBB (from 111 to MMM) as unmarked.
Repeat the following step exactly MMM times:

Choose an unmarked index iii that is adjacent to a marked index (or any index in the first iteration).
Append BiB_iBi​ to the end of CCC.
Mark index iii.


F(B)F(B)F(B) is defined as the lexicographically smallest†^\dag† array CCC that can be obtained through this process.
Stack has a permutation‡^\ddag‡ PPP of length NNN.

Let Qi=F(P[1,i])Q_i = F(P[1,i])Qi​=F(P[1,i]). He wants you to construct an array AAA of length NNN such that:


For each 1≤i≤N1\le i\le N1≤i≤N, QAiQ_{A_i}QAi​​ is the ithi^{th}ith lexicographically smallest array among the set of arrays Qi (1≤i≤N)Q_i \ (1\le i \le N)Qi​ (1≤i≤N) .
†^\dag†An array XXX is lexicographically smaller than an array YYY if one of the following holds:


XXX is a prefix of YYY and X≠YX \ne YX=Y;
In the first position where XXX and YYY differ, Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi​<Yi​.
‡^\ddag‡A permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once. P[1,j]P[1,j]P[1,j] denotes the subarray [P1,P2,…,Pj][P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_j][P1​,P2​,…,Pj​] of permutation PPP.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of the permutation PPP.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1,P_2,\ldots,P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ representing the permutation PPP.




Output Format
For each test case, print NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2,…ANA_1, A_2, \ldots A_NA1​,A2​,…AN​, denoting the array AAA.
constraints: All elements of PPP are distinct.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
